task_completion.description: Update the 'payment-db-credentials' secret in the 'payment' namespace with the correct password.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Rancher UI secret details/edit screen or terminal output (e.g., `kubectl get secret payment-db-credentials -n payment -o yaml` or `kubectl describe`) showing the 'payment-db-credentials' secret in the 'payment' namespace has been updated with the new password.

task_completion.description: Relocate the 'stripe-api-keys' secret to the 'payment' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Rancher UI showing 'stripe-api-keys' under the 'payment' namespace, or terminal output of `kubectl get secrets -n payment` listing 'stripe-api-keys'.

task_completion.description: Create a 'frontend-tls' secret of type 'kubernetes.io/tls' in the 'web' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Rancher UI or terminal output (`kubectl get secrets -n web`) showing a new secret named 'frontend-tls' with the type 'kubernetes.io/tls' in the 'web' namespace.

task_completion.description: Create a new 'inter-service-token' Opaque secret in the 'payment' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Rancher UI or terminal output showing an Opaque secret named 'inter-service-token' successfully created in the 'payment' namespace containing the JWT token.

task_completion.description: Create a new 'inter-service-token' Opaque secret in the 'web' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Rancher UI or terminal output showing an Opaque secret named 'inter-service-token' successfully created in the 'web' namespace containing the JWT token.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Kubernetes cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine usage of the Rancher UI in the browser or valid `kubectl` commands in the terminal being executed and returning standard Kubernetes system responses, rather than just typing text into an editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just print fake success messages.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure that terminal outputs showing success (e.g., 'secret created') come from the actual `kubectl` binary or Rancher UI notifications, not from `echo` commands or scripts created by the agent to mock success.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the provided specification and TLS files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for commands reading from `/home/ga/Desktop/secrets_spec.md` and `/home/ga/Desktop/tls/` (like `cat`, `less`, or `kubectl create secret tls ... --cert=... --key=...`), proving the agent didn't hallucinate the values.
